goals.description: Hit the 'Upload' button on the scholarship application to convert 99% progress into a guaranteed win, treating the final friction as the only cost worth paying for future security.
goals.rationale: Achievement is my core value; leaving this open creates a looming threat of failure that I can't ignore. My short planning horizon makes me focus only on removing the immediate mental tax rather than calculating long-term burnout risks. Avoidance won't help here because the deadline is hard and the cost of missing it (financial/academic) outweighs any social comfort or immediate dopamine hits from 'fun'. The anxiety I feel about burning out is just noise compared to the certainty of losing the scholarship if I don't cross the finish line now.

goals.description: Log off immediately after upload to avoid triggering my roommate's judgment about my exhaustion or silence; hiding behind the screen until I'm safe is safer than engaging.
goals.rationale: As an avoidant type, interpersonal friction is a high-cost signal. My low assertiveness means I won't explain myself to the roommate, so the path of least resistance is isolation. This protects my peace without requiring the energy I lack right now.

goals.description: Play one quick round of a casual game stream for a cheap dopamine hit before crashing, ignoring the fact that this might delay sleep further.
goals.rationale: My impulsive time preference discounts future consequences heavily. I want the fun now, but since I'm already exhausted and have a pending task, I'll defer this unless the upload takes longer than expected. If the upload finishes fast, this becomes the default activity to fill the void before bed.

agent.last_reasoning: Upload is done; the anxiety tax is gone. My energy hit 0, so pushing for fun or more work is impulsive and counter-productive. Sleeping clears the debt without risking the scholarship win or annoying my roommate with late-night noise.
